steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: On the left of the blank is the noun song at the end of an independent clause; on the right is a noun phrase (a title in quotation marks).

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule for appositives
steps.content: When a noun is immediately followed by a restrictive appositive—one that identifies or specifies the noun—no punctuation is used. Here, the title “A Tear” is restrictive because it specifies which song we're discussing. Commas would set off a non-restrictive appositive (extra information), semicolons separate independent clauses, and colons introduce elaborations but aren't standard before titles. That matches choice D — D is correct.
